signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme
signals.text: NZD/CHF at 0.4707 — NZD outperformance anchors the G10 scorecard as the sole currency with a clear OIS hike bias (+1.064% avg). CHF remains the G10 laggard (-0.544% avg) amid a -0.62% real policy rate. The 250bp NZD/CHF carry advantage and RBNZ hiking stance drive the 1.608% G10 spread. NZD/USD technicals show the advance stalling at SMA clusters, but the cross theme remains dominant while risk-on sentiment persists.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Options Skew + BoJ Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.11 — Pair holds above the 160.00 psychological level despite softer US CPI data. While 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid, the options market signals caution with a -1.42 25d RR (moderate put skew) and elevated VRP (+3.6%). JPY COT LF net short -104,231 remains crowded; however, positive JPY CESI (+10) and Middle East geopolitical risks introduce sharp reversal potential if BoJ signals a hawkish shift on July 31.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6985 — COT LF net long +24,098 (63.8%) shows institutional backing for the 60bp AUD carry premium. However, a -1.09 25d RR (put skew) and extreme +5.7% VRP (IV 13.1% vs HV30 7.5%) suggest the market is paying heavily for downside protection. Persistent AUD data misses (CESI -12) contradict the bullish positioning, signaling a potential long-unwind if global risk sentiment shifts. Watch 0.7000 for rejection.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + BoC Policy Report
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4047 — The pair exhibits a significant correlation break vs WTI Oil (z = -2.07σ), with the -0.888 correlation deviating from the +0.045 norm. CAD strength (1W avg +0.785%) is decoupling from energy prices ahead of today's BoC Monetary Policy Report. COT LF net short -87,846 (22.6% long) reflects extreme bearishness that may face a squeeze if Governor Macklem maintains a restrictive real rate (+0.19%) stance.
